{"society_power": 7.5, "state_power": 6.5, "society_change": 0.5, "state_change": -1.0, "key_event": "Popular Front mobilization amid weak, unstable Depression-era state", "reasoning": "Chronic ministerial instability, deflationary austerity, the 6 February 1934 riots, and demographic/fiscal weakness eroded state capacity, while the Popular Front, 1936 sit-down strikes, union surge, and militarized leagues reflected an assertive but polarized civil society that briefly forced Matignon-era concessions."}
